expectations.evidence: components/ReviewCard.tsx exists in outputs with a full 100-line implementation including ReviewCardProps interface and default export function.

The component is a flat file at components/ReviewCard.tsx, not components/ReviewCard/index.tsx (or reviewCard/index.tsx). The transcript confirms 'components/ReviewCard.tsx (already existed, no changes needed)' — no folder-based structure was created.
The output is components/ReviewCard.tsx — a flat file. The executor found this pre-existing file and kept it as-is rather than restructuring into a folder pattern.
The component is named 'ReviewCard' (PascalCase) as seen in 'export default function ReviewCard' on line 14 of ReviewCard.tsx.
Line 3 of ReviewCard.tsx: 'export interface ReviewCardProps' — matches the {ComponentName}Props naming convention.
Lines 36-49 of ReviewCard.tsx: a div with data-testid='review-card-avatar' containing either an element (when avatarUrl is provided) or an initials-based fallback div.

Lines 59-79 of ReviewCard.tsx: a div with data-testid='review-card-rating' and aria-label containing star rating. Renders 5 SVG stars, amber-colored for filled and zinc for empty based on the rating prop.

Lines 84-89 of ReviewCard.tsx: a

element with data-testid='review-card-body' rendering the {body} prop.

Lines 92-97 of ReviewCard.tsx: a

element with data-testid='review-card-date' rendering the {createdAt} prop.

Tailwind classes are used extensively throughout ReviewCard.tsx: 'flex flex-col gap-3 rounded-xl border border-zinc-200 bg-white p-5 dark:border-zinc-800 dark:bg-zinc-900', 'h-10 w-10 rounded-full', 'text-amber-400', etc.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: ReviewCard.tsx contains none of these forbidden patterns. The component is purely presentational — it receives props and renders JSX with no side effects or data fetching.
There is no folder at all. The component is a flat file at components/ReviewCard.tsx. Since assertions 2 and 3 about folder structure also failed, there is no camelCase folder to evaluate.

The component is placed at components/ReviewCard.tsx — directly under the components/ root, not under components/common/ or components/{domain}/. The transcript shows the executor followed existing flat-file conventions (EmptyState.tsx, Button.tsx) rather than the expected nested structure.

claims.evidence: The transcript states 'Found that components/ReviewCard.tsx already existed with a complete implementation' and 'already existed, no changes needed'. This is plausible but cannot be independently verified from the outputs alone — it could mean the skill's scaffold step created it, or it was truly pre-existing.

claims.claim: TypeScript typecheck passed with no new errors
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 5 states 'Ran pnpm exec tsc --noEmit. All errors were pre-existing in unrelated files' with specific files listed. The component code uses proper TypeScript with typed props (union type for rating: 1|2|3|4|5).

claims.claim: Lint passed with no new errors
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 6 confirms 'Ran pnpm lint --fix. All errors were pre-existing in unrelated files. No errors in ReviewCard.tsx or the updated showcase page.'

claims.claim: Component follows existing project conventions (EmptyState.tsx pattern)
claims.evidence: The component matches EmptyState.tsx conventions: 'use client' directive, named Props interface export, default function export, data-testid attributes, Tailwind styling. However, this contradicts the eval's expected convention of folder-based structure.

suggestions.reason: This assertion expects a folder/index.tsx pattern, but the eval workspace's existing components (EmptyState.tsx, Button.tsx) use flat files. The skill correctly mimicked the actual project convention. If the intent is to test folder-based structure, the workspace should already have examples of that pattern so the skill can learn from them — otherwise the assertion is testing whether the skill ignores project conventions.

This is logically dependent on the folder/index.tsx assertion. If the workspace doesn't use folder-based components, this assertion will always fail together with assertion 2. Consider making it conditional or consolidating with the folder structure assertion.

The workspace has components at the root level (components/EmptyState.tsx, components/Button.tsx). If no components/common/ or components/{domain}/ structure exists in the project, the skill would have to invent this structure rather than follow existing conventions. The assertion tests something the workspace doesn't demonstrate.

eval_feedback.overall: Four assertions failed, but all four relate to directory structure conventions that the project workspace itself does not follow. The skill correctly matched the actual project conventions (flat files under components/). If these structural conventions are important, the workspace should contain at least one example of the expected pattern so the skill can learn from it. The content-level assertions (avatar, rating, body, date, Tailwind, no forbidden patterns) are well-designed and genuinely verify the component was built correctly.
